articles.title: Tata Sierra EV launched at ₹18.79L; up to 665 km range, here's everything you need to know
articles.description: Tata Motors has officially launched the Tata Sierra EV in India, with prices starting at ₹18.79 lakh (ex-showroom). Available with 63 kWh and 75 kWh battery packs, the electric SUV offers a claimed MIDC range of up to 665 km. It also introduces several segment-first features to compete with the Mahindra BE 6, Mahindra XEV 9e, Hyundai Creta Electric, Maruti Suzuki e Vitara, and other electric SUVs in India's fast-growing EV market.

articles.title: Inside $5.5 billion ‘Robot Park’ where humanoid robots learn to work before getting jobs
articles.description: Apptronik's CEO calls the facility a "data factory," where every task performed by humanoid robots helps improve the AI powering future generations.

articles.title: Anthropic unveils Claude Sonnet 5 with better performance, lower costs
articles.description: Anthropic has launched Claude Sonnet 5, an AI model with enhanced autonomous capabilities, improved reasoning, and stronger support for business automation.

articles.title: Agnikul, ICEYE join hands to build, launch SAR satellites from India
articles.description: Agnikul, ICEYE join hands to build, launch SAR satellites from India

articles.title: AI skills power nearly two-thirds of new GCC hiring in India
articles.description: India's white-collar hiring saw a dip in June, yet its Global Capability Centres (GCCs) are booming, especially in AI. GCCs are shifting from cost-cutting to innovation hubs, with technology and BFSI sectors leading the charge. Bengaluru remains the top hub, but tier-2 cities are rapidly emerging as key talent centers, showcasing India's growing strategic importance for multinational corporations.

articles.title: ‘Main school student nahi hu...’: Gen Z employee rejects manager's bizarre demand for sick leave
articles.description: The screenshot, shared online by Vishal, a X user, shows the employee informing his manager that he would not be coming to work because his fever had worsened

articles.title: Neon acquires Luca Guadagnino's OpenAI movie 'Artificial' after Amazon MGM exit
articles.description: Neon has secured global rights to Luca Guadagnino's "Artificial," a film detailing Sam Altman's brief ouster from OpenAI. Starring Andrew Garfield as Altman, the movie explores the AI leadership drama and is being positioned for an awards campaign. The feature, previously dropped by Amazon MGM Studios, boasts a notable cast and was shot in San Francisco and Italy, promising a compelling look at a pivotal tech moment.

articles.title: Centre asks Meta to pause WhatsApp username rollout; seeks reply in three days
articles.description: It has directed Meta to furnish a detailed explanation of the feature within three days. The company has been asked not to roll out the feature until consultation on the matter is over, sources told ET.

articles.title: EPF Scheme 2026: Your provident fund is getting simpler, smarter and more digital
articles.description: India's provident fund system has undergone a significant overhaul with the introduction of the Employees' Provident Fund Scheme, 2026, replacing the 1952 version. This modernization prioritizes digital compliance and simplified processes, aligning with new labor codes. Key changes include easier partial withdrawals for members and enhanced governance for employers, aiming for wider social security coverage and improved benefit delivery.

articles.title: ‘He Has AI Psychosis And We’re F**ked’: Employee Says Boss Trusts Claude AI More Than His Own Team
articles.description: An employee claims their boss has started trusting an AI chatbot over the team, using its predictions to judge performance even when they don’t match real-world situations

articles.title: Apple price hike fear: Why iPhone 17 buyers may not want to wait
articles.description: Flipkart has cut the iPhone 17 price, with card and exchange offers pushing it below ₹55,000 for some buyers as concerns grow over a possible future Apple price hike across India.

articles.title: ‘Bro Tracked The Tracker’: X User Claims They Found CBSE Whistleblower Nisarga’s Hinge Profile By Scraping 500+ Accounts
articles.description: The internet is divided between calling it “smart effort” and “crazy stalker behaviour” after a man claimed he tracked down Nisarga’s alleged Hinge profile

articles.title: Royal Enfield sales up 27 pc to 1,14,032 motorcycles in June
articles.description: Royal Enfield revved up sales in June 2026, reporting a significant 27% surge to 1,14,032 motorcycles. This growth is attributed to strong demand for their updated models. Adding to the excitement, the company has begun delivering its groundbreaking electric motorcycle, the Flying Flea C6, in Bengaluru, with plans for a wider rollout. Management expresses optimism for continued sales momentum.

articles.title: Sedemac Mechatronics: India's play on automotive intelligence

articles.title: Decoding BPM 3.0 at the intersection of agility, adaptability, and AI
articles.description: The dawn of BPM 3.0 is here and it is marked by what industry experts term as “process reimagination”. For the three-and-a-half to close to four decades, India has dominated the ITES industry; today, what we are witnessing is an AI-driven shift, where processes are being reimagined with stronger ownership of outcomes, machine-human collaboration, and a more agile approach. Here’s a detailed overview.

articles.title: Digital India made India a global model of innovation, says CM Dhami
articles.description: Uttarakhand CM Pushkar Singh Dhami praised Digital India on its 11th anniversary, highlighting how it has transformed India into a global innovation model. The country has over 1.03B internet connections and has connected 220,000 Gram Panchayats.

articles.title: Ola Electric doubles QoQ growth in Q1 FY26; June vehicle registrations at 16,144 units
articles.description: Ola Electric witnessed a significant surge in electric two-wheeler registrations during the April-June quarter, nearly doubling from the previous period. This impressive growth, reaching 43,719 vehicles, is attributed to enhanced retail operations, better product availability, and consistent customer demand. The company registered its highest monthly sales in June, signaling a strong recovery in market momentum and underscoring its commitment to expanding EV adoption in India.

articles.title: Ola Electric Mobility rises after vehicle registrations nearly double in Q1 FY26
articles.description: Ola Electric Mobility added 2.79% to Rs 45.01 after the company announced that it has registered 43,719 vehicles in Q1 FY26, nearly doubling from 22,252 vehicles in Q4 FY25, according to VAHAN data.

articles.description: The measure, if approved, will close a "loophole" and "protect Americans from electronic devices that have ​been determined to pose unacceptable risks to the national ⁠security of the ‌United States," the FCC said in a statement.

articles.title: Major automakers record strong June sales on steady domestic demand, rising exports
articles.description: Indian auto manufacturers celebrated robust June sales, fueled by strong domestic demand and surging exports. Maruti Suzuki reported over 200,000 units sold, while Mahindra & Mahindra saw a significant 37% growth with over 106,000 vehicles. Hyundai Motor India also posted strong figures, overcoming a temporary production setback due to a supplier issue. These impressive numbers highlight a thriving automotive sector.

articles.title: Apple iPhone Ultra vs Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide: What we know and what to expect
articles.description: Apple's first foldable iPhone is expected to take on Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide, but the two devices will likely target different audiences.

articles.title: Gruha Jyothi Yojana: Karnataka launches door-to-door verification
articles.description: The Karnataka Energy Department began a door-to-door verification drive for Gruha Jyothi Yojana beneficiaries. The drive aims to update records for the free electricity scheme. Beneficiaries are asked to keep documents ready for ESCOM staff.

articles.title: Cyber threats now tailored for demographic groups
articles.description: Cybercriminals are increasingly targeting vulnerable groups, weaponizing age and innocence. Senior citizens are falling prey to financial scams, while women face exploitation through intimate data and reputational harm. Most alarming is the sharp surge in child exploitation online, with a dramatic increase in cases involving sexually explicit material. Experts urge caution and verification in online interactions.

articles.title: Family Feud Pocket lands on Apple Arcade, with 4 more games coming later this week
articles.description: Apple Arcade subscribers can now play Family Feud Pocket , with four additional games set to join the service later this week. Here are the details.

articles.title: Live Near a Noisy xAI Data Center? How About Half Off Starlink Satellite Internet?
articles.description: SpaceX halves the price of Starlink for those living near its data centers in Memphis and Southaven, Mississippi. The company is facing lawsuits over noise and pollution in both regions.

articles.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is investing $1 billion in a new unit that places AI engineers inside client companies to speed up artificial intelligence deployment from months to days.

articles.title: New macro indicators in the works to track Viksit Bharat progress
articles.description: India is crafting a robust Viksit Bharat Sankalp indicator framework, featuring around 385 macro indicators, to meticulously track its journey towards becoming a developed nation by 2047. This data-driven mechanism, spearheaded by the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation, will assess economic, social, and infrastructure progress across key sectors. The comprehensive system aims for implementation by 2031, ensuring inclusive and sustainable development is at the forefront.

articles.title: Samsung confirms the Galaxy Ring 2
articles.description: Samsung launched the Galaxy Ring in 2024, and so you may have been wondering where its successor is. Back in May we heard that the Galaxy Ring 2 would arrive in early 2027 with improved battery life, comfort, and sensor accuracy. Now a new report says Samsung itself has confirmed that the Galaxy Ring 2 is in development, through the voice of Dr. Hon Pak, the company's head of the Digital Health team. The original Samsung Galaxy Ring He strongly hinted at upcoming iPhone pairing support, while confirming that the next Galaxy Ring will have longer battery life and upgraded health...

articles.description: The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 and Z Flip8 series are just around the corner, and the intensity of leaks grows. The latest one comes from a reputable source, revealing some Galaxy Z Fold8 key specs. If you haven't been following the story, keep in mind that the new Galaxy Z Fold8 isn't a direct successor to the Galaxy Z Fold7 from last year. It's actually the previously rumored "Galaxy Fold Wide", which will take the place of the vanilla, while the current Z Fold7's true successor will be the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra. Reportedly, Samsung decided to change the naming scheme back in...

articles.title: Nearly 45% Of Indian Homes Not EV-Ready, Need Electrical Upgrades: Report
articles.description: India's electric mobility transition has gained significant momentum over the past decade, yet access to residential charging remains deeply uneven, as per the report.

articles.description: Amazon's month-end price cut on Apple's M5 Pro MacBook Pro with an upgrade to 2TB of storage delivers a 15% discount off retail. articles.description: SEBI Executive Director Manoj Kumar said the regulator is using its SUDARSHAN tool to scan websites for non-compliant finfluencer-type ads and is exploring AI to simplify mutual fund communication.

articles.title: West Asia crisis shifted Indian EV market from 'push mode to pull mode': Tata Motors MD Shailesh Chandra
articles.description: While the entry of multiple players in the EV segment and the breaking of barriers across subsegments such as pricing, range anxiety, and charging infrastructure have helped EV growth, the West Asia war has also played a significant role, Shailesh Chandra said.
